title: Voice Notes
description: Audio notes captured in the Voice app — recorded in the browser Dictaphone or imported by dropping an audio/video file — each stored as a `files` row alongside an auto-generated, editable transcript so the note is readable and searchable without playback.

ai.purpose: Stores dictaphone recordings: the audio blob (as a `files` row) plus the transcribed text so the note is searchable and readable without playback.
ai.howItWorks: The `VoiceDictaphonePrefab` records audio in the browser, calls `/voice-api/api/stt` to transcribe, then on save uploads the audio to core's `/api/files/uploadDirect` and creates a `voice_notes` row linking `audioFileId` to the resulting file and storing the (optionally edited) `transcript`.
ai.manipulation: Create rows through the dictaphone prefab. Editing `transcript` later is fine — it's just the cached text. Don't edit `audioFileId` unless you mean to swap the underlying recording.

outcomes.how: Open the Dictaphone, press Record, stop, optionally edit the transcript, click Save. A `voice_notes` row is created with `audioFileId` pointing at the new `files` record.

warnings: Deleting a `voice_notes` row does NOT delete the underlying `files` record. Delete the file separately if needed.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short label for this note. Defaults to the capture timestamp for recordings, or the source filename for imported media.

audioFileId.title: Audio
audioFileId.description: The note's audio, stored as a row in core's `files` collection. Dictaphone recordings keep their original captured blob; imported media is transcoded to WAV before upload.

transcript.title: Transcript
transcript.description: Text of what was said, auto-generated by the speech-to-text engine. Editable in the Dictaphone before saving, and editable afterwards on the record (it is just cached text — editing it does not re-run STT).

language.title: Language
language.description: ISO 639-1 language code detected during transcription (e.g. `en`), or the hint the caller supplied. May be absent if STT did not return one.

durationMs.title: Duration (ms)
durationMs.description: Length of the audio in milliseconds, measured by the backend during transcode. Read-only.

mimeType.title: MIME type
mimeType.description: MIME type of the stored audio, cached here for fast list rendering without fetching the file. `audio/wav` for imported media (always transcoded); the recorded blob's type for Dictaphone captures.

title: Voices
description: Catalog of cloneable TTS voices available through the Voice app. Each row pairs a reference recording with its verbatim transcript; the voice-api reconciler clones that pair into an OmniVoice voice profile and stores the resulting profile id, so playback (Speaker, Studio, Settings) can synthesise speech in that voice.

ai.purpose: Stores the voice profiles users can choose from for text-to-speech playback. Each row pairs a reference audio file + transcript that voice-api uses to provision an OmniVoice voice profile.
ai.howItWorks: Source of truth lives here. `voice-api` runs a reconciler that, for each row with `audioFileId` + `refText`, downloads the audio from core's `files` collection and POSTs it to OmniVoice `/profiles`, then writes the resulting `profile_id` back to `profileId` on this row. `VoiceSpeakerPrefab` looks up the user's preferred voice (stored on `user_ui_configs.voice.selectedVoiceId`) and forwards `profileId` to OmniVoice `/generate`. Because the reference audio is persisted in core (not in the OmniVoice container's volume), profiles survive stack rebuilds — the reconciler re-provisions them on startup.
ai.manipulation: To add a voice, upload a 3+ second reference WAV via the standard core file uploader (which creates a `files` record), then create a `voice_voices` row with `displayName`, `audioFileId` set to the file's `_id`, and `refText` set to the audio's transcript. Leave `profileId` empty — the reconciler fills it in. Use `enabled=false` to hide a voice from pickers without deleting it. `order` controls picker sort.

outcomes.outcome: Add a voice from a reference audio sample
outcomes.how: Upload the WAV via the standard files uploader to get a `files._id`. Create a `voice_voices` row with `audioFileId` set to that id, `refText` set to what's spoken in the audio, plus `displayName`, `language`, `gender`. The reconciler clones the profile in OmniVoice and back-fills `profileId` within ~1s.
Set `enabled=false`. The catalog row stays for traceability and the reconciler stops touching it.

warnings: Don't manually edit `profileId` — it's backend-managed by the reconciler. Editing it points the row at a stale OmniVoice profile that may not exist after a rebuild.
`audioFileId` MUST point at a real `files` record the voice-api functional user can read (admin/user group, not private). Private files won't be visible to the reconciler.
Do not delete a voice that users have selected as their preference — flip `enabled=false` instead, so the prefab can fall back gracefully.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable unique slug for this voice. Auto-derived as `<title-slug>-<noteId6>` when a voice is created from a voice note; otherwise set it yourself. Must be unique across the catalog.

displayName.title: Display name
displayName.description: User-facing label shown in the voice picker, Settings prefab, and Studio dropdown.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional free-text notes about the voice's character or intended use case. Shown in the Settings prefab.

engine.title: Engine
engine.description: TTS engine that backs this voice. Only `omnivoice` is supported today; the enum is kept open for future engines.

profileId.title: Profile ID
profileId.description: OmniVoice profile identifier this voice maps to. Backend-managed: the voice-api reconciler clones a profile in OmniVoice from `audioFileId` + `refText` and writes the returned id here. Leave empty when creating a voice; do not hand-edit (a stale id may not exist after an OmniVoice volume reset).

provisionedFromAudioFileId.title: Provisioned from audio
provisionedFromAudioFileId.description: Backend-managed drift marker recording the `audioFileId` that produced the current `profileId`. When it no longer matches `audioFileId`, the reconciler deletes the stale OmniVoice profile and re-clones from the new audio.

provisionedFromRefText.title: Provisioned from refText
provisionedFromRefText.description: Backend-managed drift marker recording the `refText` that produced the current `profileId`. A mismatch with `refText` triggers re-provisioning.

provisionedFromSeed.title: Provisioned seed
provisionedFromSeed.description: Backend-managed drift marker: the deterministic seed (derived from audioFileId + refText) pinned on the OmniVoice profile so repeat TTS calls reproduce the same timbre. A missing value on an existing profile triggers re-provisioning.

audioFileId.title: Reference audio
audioFileId.description: Reference recording (a `files` row) that OmniVoice clones to create this voice. 3+ seconds of clean single-speaker speech recommended. Must be readable by the voice-backend functional user — voices created from a voice note have the file's groupIds widened automatically.

refText.title: Reference text
refText.description: Verbatim transcript of `audioFileId`. OmniVoice uses it to align the cloned voice — it should match the spoken audio exactly for best quality. Required (alongside audio) before the reconciler will provision a profile.

language.title: Language
language.description: Language hint passed to OmniVoice for this voice. An ISO 639-1 code (e.g. `en`, `es`, `de`) or `Auto` to autodetect from the reference audio. Defaults to `Auto` when omitted.

gender.title: Gender
gender.description: Declared voice gender. Informational metadata for pickers; does not affect synthesis.

style.title: Style tags
style.description: Free-form descriptive tags (e.g. `warm`, `news`, `narrator`) for filtering/labelling in the picker.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: When false, the voice is hidden from pickers and the reconciler stops touching it, without deleting the catalog row.

order.title: Order
order.description: Ascending sort position in voice pickers and the Settings list.

groupIds.title: Groups
groupIds.description: Row-security scope. Must include the voice-backend functional user's group so the reconciler can read the reference audio and provision the profile.
